candidates.evidence_quote: Goodwill and other indefinite-lived intangible assets are not amortized and are tested for impairment annually as of the first day of our fiscal fourth quarter

Margins and adjusted diluted net income per share finished toward the higher end of our expectations, reflecting the resilience of our business and strong operational execution.
Fourth quarter fiscal 2025 includes $17.3 million of gift card breakage revenue as a result of a change in historical redemption patterns.
partially offset by a non-recurring $7.9 million write-down of gift card inventory
During the fourth quarter, we opened seven new restaurants and delivered approximately 7% unit growth for the year.

The Company now expects to open as many as 26 new restaurants in fiscal 2026, including as many as six The Cheesecake Factory restaurants, six to seven North Italia locations, six to seven Flower Child locations and as many as eight FRC restaurants.
closed four restaurants, including two Cheesecake Factory restaurants, one Grand Lux Cafe, and one FRC location
approved an increase of 5.0 million shares to the existing repurchase program raising the total authorization to 66 million shares
the Board approved a $0.03 increase in the quarterly dividend to $0.30 per share
Despite a more challenging operating environment across the restaurant industry, including weather-related impacts, revenue for the quarter finished within our expected range.

notes: No fiscal_kpis in this chunk (KPIs ride on chunk_001 only); every candidate is grounded in event text.
10-K event is part 6 of 6 (critical accounting estimates + impairment/lease detail); names coined only from this visible part.

Namesake-brand comp kept as company-wide same_store_sales because 'The Cheesecake Factory' is also the legal company name (banned inside driver names); North Italia brand comp kept as its own driver per brand-level rule.

Skipped as vague/accounting-method/single-instance boilerplate (14): lease term judgments, incremental borrowing rate, discount-rate assumptions, share price performance factor, foreign exchange rates, government shutdowns, unemployment rates, landlord/tenant economic health, pandemic dining restrictions, international market acceptance, brand acceptance of newer concepts, interest rate fluctuations, bare 'heightened inflation', new-unit permitting timing.
